sub_pixel_system.notes: Speeds measured in pixels:subpixels format where 256 subpixels equals one pixel

special_mechanics.name: Air Drag
special_mechanics.description: All characters except SkyCutter decelerate in air by 0:1 per frame regardless of jump source. If speed falls below 2:95, it resets to 2:160.

special_mechanics.name: Enemy Bounce Boost
special_mechanics.description: Bouncing off enemies provides a speed boost. Multiple bounces in succession possible. Mechanics under investigation.

special_mechanics.name: Surface Friction - Rubber
special_mechanics.description: Walking on rubber decreases max speed: reduces run speed by 0:128 and walk speed modification.

special_mechanics.name: Surface Acceleration - Ice
special_mechanics.description: Walking on ice speeds up character by approximately 0:4 per frame.

special_mechanics.name: SkyCutter - Variable Gravity
special_mechanics.description: When forward or B (jump) held, gravity affects SkyCutter less. Gravity behavior changes based on input.

physics_values.note: Gravity mechanics described as mysterious, requires further investigation

special_mechanics.name: SkyCutter - Horizontal Speed Control
special_mechanics.description: Horizontal speed depends only on whether A (run) is held or not.

special_mechanics.name: SkyCutter - Landing Penalties
special_mechanics.description: Landing, running off ledge, or ducking stops SkyCutter for one frame if board turned down. Jumping reduces speed to 3 pixels/frame. No penalty if board up.

special_mechanics.name: MicroMax - Wall Cling & Climbing
special_mechanics.description: 1x1 block sized character that can cling to walls. Zig-zagging faster in 1-2 block width tunnels than single-wall climbing.

special_mechanics.name: MicroMax - Increased Air Control
special_mechanics.description: Can adjust jump height/distance by holding or releasing B even after jump peak, unlike other characters.

special_mechanics.name: MicroMax - Cannon Avoidance
special_mechanics.description: Fits between horizontal spikes from cannon blocks (normal characters fit between vertical spikes).

special_mechanics.name: Cyclone - Flight
special_mechanics.description: Can fly by pressing C repeatedly. Flying speed slower than running, but can maintain higher speed if flying off ledge or after enemy bounce.

special_mechanics.name: Red Stealth - Strike While Jumping Bug
special_mechanics.description: Can strike in air (normally impossible) by starting strike with C then jumping mid-animation. Strike animation shows but damage applies frames later.

special_mechanics.name: Maniaxe - Rapid Throw Bug
special_mechanics.description: Jump during throw animation to cancel it and throw again quickly. Best method: B (jump) 1 frame, C (throw) 4 frames, repeated.

tier_notes: T2 empirical data from TASVideos speedrun documentation. Speed values in pixel:subpixel format well-documented for multiple characters. Gravity and acceleration values not explicitly numerically documented; qualitative descriptions available for special character mechanics. Jump velocity values only documented for Red Stealth, Cyclone, MicroMax, and SkyCutter forms, not base Kid Chameleon.
sources_used: TASVideos - Kid Chameleon Game Resources (movement speeds, air drag, surface physics, character-specific mechanics)
